children.text: an amendment to the articles of incorporation of a Native Corporation authorized by subsections (g) and (h) of , subsection (d)(1)(B) of this section, or ;section 1606 of this titlesection 1629c of this title
a resolution authorized by ;section 1629d(a)(2) of this title
a resolution to establish a Settlement Trust; or
a resolution to convey all or substantially all of the assets of a Native Corporation to a Settlement Trust pursuant to ;section 1629e(a)(1) of this title

An amendment or resolution described in subsection (a) may be approved by the board of directors of a Native Corporation in accordance with its bylaws. If the board approves the amendment or resolution, it shall direct that the amendment or resolution be submitted to a vote of the shareholders at the next annual meeting or at a special meeting (if the board, at its discretion, schedules such special meeting). One or more such amendments or resolutions may be submitted to the shareholders and voted upon at one meeting.

children.label: A
children.text: A written notice (including a proxy statement if required under applicable law), setting forth the amendment or resolution approved pursuant to paragraph (1) (and, at the discretion of the board, a summary of the changes to be effected) together with any amendment or resolution submitted pursuant to subsection (c) and the statements described therein shall be sent, not less than fifty days nor more than sixty days prior to the meeting of the shareholders, by first-class mail or hand-delivered to each shareholder of record entitled to vote at his or her address as it appears in the records of the Native Corporation. The corporation may also communicate with its shareholders at any time and in any manner authorized by the laws of the State.

children.label: i
children.text: land conveyed to the corporation pursuant to or any other land used as a cemetery;section 1613(h)(1) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: exempt from real estate taxation pursuant to ; andsection 1636(d)(1)(A) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: used by the shareholders of the corporation for subsistence uses (as defined in ); orsection 3113 of title 16

children.label: iii
children.text: land or interest in land which the board of directors believes to be only of speculative value;

children.label: C
children.text: If the board of directors determines, for quorum purposes or otherwise, that a previously-noticed meeting must be postponed or adjourned, it may, by giving notice to the shareholders, set a new date for such meeting not more than forty-five days later than the original date without sending the shareholders a new written notice (or a new summary of changes to be effected). If the new date is more than forty-five days later than the original date, however, a new written notice (and a new summary of changes to be effected if such a summary was originally sent pursuant to subparagraph (A)), shall be sent or delivered to shareholders not less than thirty days nor more than forty-five days prior to the new date.

children.label: A
children.text: With respect to an amendment authorized by or or an amendment authorizing the issuance of stock subject to the restrictions provided by , the holders of shares representing at least 25 per centum of the total voting power of a Native Corporation may petition the board of directors to submit such amendment to a vote of the shareholders in accordance with the provisions of this section.section 1606(g)(1)(B) of this titlesection 1629c(b) of this titlesection 1606(g)(2)(B)(iii) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the board agrees with such petition, the board shall submit the amendment and either the proponents’ statement or its own statement in support of the amendment to the shareholders for a vote, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the board disagrees with the petition for any reason, the board shall submit the amendment and the proponents’ statement to the shareholders for a vote and may, at its discretion, submit an opposing statement or an alternative amendment.

Paragraph (1) shall not apply to a Native Corporation that on or before the date one year after , elects application of in lieu of . Until , paragraph (1) shall not apply to a Native Corporation that elects application of in lieu of . Insofar as they are not inconsistent with this section, the laws of the State shall govern any shareholder right of petition for Native Corporations.February 3, 1988December 18, 1991section 1629c(d) of this titlesection 1629c(b) of this titlesection 1629c(c) of this titlesection 1629c(b) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a majority of the total voting power of the corporation, or

children.label: B
children.text: a level of the total voting power of the corporation greater than a majority (but not greater than two-thirds of the total voting power of the corporation) if the corporation establishes such a level by an amendment to its articles of incorporation.

A Native Corporation in amending its articles of incorporation pursuant to to authorize the issuance of a new class or series of stock may provide that a majority (or more than a majority) of the shares of such class or series must vote in favor of an amendment or resolution described in subsection (a) (other than an amendment authorized by ) in order for such amendment or resolution to be approved.section 1606(g)(2) of this titlesection 1629c of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a majority of the shares present or represented by proxy at the meeting relating to the resolution or amendment to articles of incorporation; or

children.label: B
children.text: an amount of shares greater than a majority of the shares present or represented by proxy at the meeting relating to the resolution or amendment to articles of incorporation (but not greater than two-thirds of the total voting power of the corporation) if the corporation establishes such a level by an amendment to its articles of incorporation.

children.heading: Voting power
children.label: e Voting power
children.text: For the purposes of this section, the determination of total voting power of a Native Corporation shall include all outstanding shares of stock that carry voting rights except shares that are not permitted to vote on the amendment or resolution in question because of restrictions in the articles of incorporation of the corporation.

children.heading: Substantially all of the assets
children.label: f Substantially all of the assets
children.text: For purposes of this section and , a Native Corporation shall be considered to be transferring all or substantially all of its assets to a Settlement Trust only if such assets represent two-thirds or more of the fair market value of the Native Corporation’s total assets.section 1629e of this title
